description_long: This object is a set of four standard traffic lights, each with three circular signal lenses arranged vertically. The lights are encased in robust, black rectangular housings with slightly protruding visors above each lens to shield from sunlight and improve visibility. The lenses display the classic red, yellow, and green colors, with each light showing a different illuminated state. The rear and side views reveal the depth of the housing and the cylindrical shape of the lens compartments. The construction appears to be primarily metal with glass or polycarbonate lenses, and the units are designed for mounting on poles or overhead structures in urban environments. No pedestrian signals or control boxes are visible, and the design is typical for vehicle traffic management at intersections.
description: A set of four vertically stacked, black traffic lights with red, yellow, and green signal lenses, each in a robust housing. The lights are designed for urban intersections and show different illuminated states.
description_view_0: Front view showing four traffic lights with visible red, yellow, and green lenses, each light displaying a different illuminated color.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and cylindrical protrusion of the lens compartments, with no signal colors visible.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the back panels of the four traffic lights, with no illuminated lenses or signal details visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the cylindrical lens compartments from the other side.
